content: The Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE) was established on July 5, 2001, under the auspices of the Mohamed Sathak Trust. It is recognized by AICTE New Delhi and is affiliated with Anna University Chennai and the Government of Tamil Nadu. MSAJCE is committed to enhancing its facilities to deliver quality technical education that meets both industrial and societal demands through skill-based training and state-of-the-art amenities.

The college is located within the lush 70-acre campus of the SIPCOT IT Park in Siruseri, surrounded by over 100 leading IT companies, including TCS, CTS, Infosys, and others. It boasts excellent infrastructure, featuring spacious classrooms, drawing halls, laboratories, seminar halls, computer facilities with internet and Wi-Fi connectivity, a library, workshops, an auditorium, and a playground. The campus is easily accessible by road, rail, and air from different parts of the country.

A dedicated team of qualified and experienced faculty members enhances the educational experience, emphasizing the importance of academic, co-curricular, and extracurricular activities. MSAJCE offers various training programs aimed at the professional and holistic development of its students.

Choosing MSAJCE provides numerous advantages, including its prime location amid a vibrant IT ecosystem and access to advanced facilities and industry collaborative technology centers. The academic process promotes smart learning, encouraging students to engage in research projects, industrial hackathons, and project competitions that enhance competency-based learning.

Additionally, the college organizes professional society activities, clubs, and student chapters that foster innovative learning. MSAJCE also offers a Student Exchange Program for cultural learning, along with internships, industrial visits, guest lectures, and seminars/webinars conducted by industry experts to align education with industry expectations. There are communication language learning facilities available in English, German, and Japanese, as well as value-added courses and technology training to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and industry requirements.
The college has a strong placement record and provides support for students pursuing higher education or entrepreneurship.

The university offers various B.E. and B.Tech programmes with a total sanctioned intake specified for each. For instance, the Civil Engineering programme has an intake of 30, with 15 seats allocated for the government quota and 15 for the management quota. Similarly, the Computer Science and Engineering programme allows for 60 students, split equally between quotas. Other offered programmes include Electronics & Communication Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, and several specializations in Computer Science.
Additionally, 5% of the seats within the approved intake are reserved for NRI candidates. If NRI seats remain unfilled, they will be allocated to general candidates based on merit.

For postgraduate courses, the university offers an M.E. in Computer Science and Engineering with an intake of 9 students, and a Structural Engineering programme with an intake of 18 students. The allocation for both programmes includes a set number of seats for government and management quotas and, similarly, 5% of seats for NRI candidates.
The university provides a Ph.D. programme in Mechanical Engineering, with specific admission criteria based on the completion of a Master’s degree or equivalent qualification.

Students who have completed Plus 2 or a diploma may apply for the Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA Counselling) conducted by the Directorate of Technical Education, Chennai. Candidates from other states such as Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Kerala will be selected based on qualifying marks (Q) in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry at the Plus Two level, calculated with a specific formula.
For assistance, particularly for students from other states, Dr. Vamsi Naga Mohan, who speaks multiple languages, is available for support.

- Mrs. I.S. Suganthi (Assistant Professor) - 7299772958, ece.suganthi@msajce-edu.in

For B.E. and B.Tech programmes, candidates must pass the HSC (Academic) or its equivalent with a minimum average percentage in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry. Specific cut-offs vary by community, with the General category requiring 45% and other categories ranging from 40% to 45%.
For direct second-year admission, candidates with a Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Science must meet minimum eligibility marks, which also vary by community.
For M.E. and M.Tech programmes, candidates are admitted based on their qualifications in relevant disciplines, with admission conducted through the Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET).
Ph.D. candidates must possess a Master’s degree in the relevant field or a Bachelor's degree with 15 years of R&D experience, alongside publications or patents.
This comprehensive admission information outlines the various programmes and qualifications needed for aspirants looking to join the institution.

The Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science offers a Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) program in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS), designed to produce graduates proficient in the core technologies of AI, data mining, machine learning, and big data analytics. This field harnesses sophisticated algorithms that enable machines to replicate human intelligence processes such as visual perception and decision-making. In addition, data science applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets for meaningful insights, leveraging both structured and unstructured data forms.

Since its inception in 2015, the B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science has gained recognition as a dynamic and lucrative career path. The program equips students with cross-disciplinary skills spanning statistics, computer science, and logic, opening up career opportunities in various sectors, including healthcare, e-commerce, and biotechnology. Positions for graduates include software engineer, data quality analyst, medical information scientist, and IT specialist, among others.

The course duration is typically four years for full-time students, with lateral entry options available for three years. It comprises eight semesters with an intake of 30 students, divided equally between government and management categories. Eligibility requires completion of the 10+2 education system with a passing score in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

The department's vision is to emerge as a center of excellence committed to sustainable practices in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science. The mission is to provide essential foundational knowledge and foster critical thinking, ethical standards, and creativity in students, preparing them for successful careers.

The program's educational objectives include preparing graduates for developing innovative software solutions, equipping them with contemporary problem-solving skills, and instilling managerial abilities for career advancement or entrepreneurship. Program-specific outcomes focus on the application of knowledge to real-time industry challenges and the creation of innovative mobile applications.

Graduates will also achieve various program outcomes, such as applying engineering knowledge to complex problems, conducting investigations using research methodologies, and demonstrating effective communication and teamwork abilities. Moreover, they will assess the societal and environmental impacts of their solutions and adhere to ethical standards in their professional practices.
The sectors of AI and Data Science in India are projected to experience explosive growth by 2025, indicating a high demand for knowledgeable professionals in these fields.
### Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning

The Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning focuses on harnessing technology to reproduce human-like behavior in machines. Artificial Intelligence (AI) enables machines to mimic human actions, while Machine Learning (ML)—a subset of AI—teaches machines to learn from past data autonomously. Together, these fields are at the forefront of developing intelligent systems capable of solving complex problems.

The Bachelor of Technology (B. Tech) in Computer Science and Engineering with a specialization in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (CSE-AIML) is designed to equip budding engineers with a comprehensive understanding of computer technologies alongside essential skills in programming and software development. This program stands out as ideal for students eager to delve into AI and ML, which are rapidly gaining importance in various domains such as healthcare, finance, agriculture, and security.

The curriculum encompasses fundamental courses in Computational Mathematics and core areas of Computer Science, paired with specialized courses in AI and ML. Students will explore diverse topics including deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision, gaining practical experience with industry-standard tools and technologies such as Python, R, and TensorFlow.

Graduates of the AIML program can look forward to exciting career pathways as positions such as Big Data Engineer, Data Scientist, and AI Engineer among others. Companies, including Microsoft, Amazon, and Goldman Sachs, actively seek candidates with AI and ML expertise. Furthermore, graduates are well-prepared for continued education or research opportunities at prestigious institutions.

- **Duration:** 4 years for Regular Entry; 3 years for Lateral Entry
- **No. of Semesters:** 8 for Regular Entry; 6 for Lateral Entry
- **Seat Allocation:** Total of 30 seats, divided equally between Government and Management categories
- **Eligibility:** Candidates must have passed the 10+2 educational system with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

The department envisions becoming a center of excellence for developing proficient engineers in AI and ML through sustainable practices. The mission focuses on imparting core knowledge and skills while fostering critical thinking, ethics, and lifelong learning necessary for industry effectiveness.

1. Graduates will be equipped to analyze, design, develop, and test innovative software solutions.
2. They will utilize modern tools for solving complex industrial and societal problems.
3. They will be prepared for leadership and entrepreneurial roles.

- Graduates will develop creative solutions utilizing emerging technologies for real-time applications.
- They will apply their knowledge to create software solutions and innovative mobile applications.
The curriculum aims to ensure graduates are proficient in:

1. Applying foundational knowledge to solve engineering problems.
2. Analyzing and formulating solutions to complex issues.
3. Designing effective engineering solutions with societal considerations.
4. Conducting thorough investigations of complex problems using research methods.
5. Utilizing modern engineering tools effectively, while understanding their limitations.
6. Assessing societal and environmental impacts of engineering solutions.
7. Upholding ethical principles in professional engineering practices.
8. Functioning effectively in individual and team settings.
9. Communicating complex engineering activities effectively to diverse audiences.
10. Understanding project management and financial aspects of engineering.
This program prepares students for a thriving future within the AI and ML domains, aligning their education with the demands of the evolving technological landscape.

The MSAJCE Alumni Association aims to cultivate a strong connection between its alumni and the Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering. This association is dedicated to fostering intellectual and emotional relationships with former students, thereby encouraging goodwill and voluntary support for future engineers. Their initiatives include providing assistance to students in need, maintaining an employment database, and organizing various college activities.

- Fostering a sense of connection to the institution.
- Renewing relationships with students.
- Actively participating in college activities.
- Offering financial assistance through scholarships.
- Organizing teaching and training sessions for current students.
- Contributing to the overall enhancement of the institution.

Dr. K. S. Srinivasan serves as the President of the association, with Mr. Ahamed Jameel A.M.T as Secretary. Other notable members include Mr. J. Abdul Rahman as Vice President and Mr. M.M. Syed Sultan Allaudeen, who serves as a Joint Secretary. Various Executive Members such as Mr. Mohamed Yasar and Ms. Misbah Khairunissa A contribute actively to the association's efforts.

Over the years, the alumni community has engaged in numerous significant programs. Notable events included the Silver Jubilee Event, which was attended by esteemed guests including Anbazhagan and Mohammed Ateeq. Additionally, alumni have played essential roles in student placements and internships across various departments.

- Alumni like Abdur Rahim Saleh, CEO of V5 Innovations, participated as guests in significant college events.
- A humble contribution was made by the alumni during the Silver Jubilee Reunion Meet in 2025, with over 500 participants celebrating together.
- The association has also honored female alumni achievers during International Women's Day, recognizing their contributions to society.
The MSAJCE Alumni Association continues to thrive, reinforcing its commitment to benefit students and the institution through active participation and generous contributions from its members.

The Department of Civil Engineering at Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering in Siruseri, Chennai, India, has been established since 2007 and is recognized for its excellence in academics, research, and various activities. Civil engineering plays a crucial role in developing infrastructure and maintaining essential systems such as roads, buildings, bridges, and airports, addressing public and private sector needs. The profession is diverse, encompassing areas like structural engineering, transport engineering, and environmental engineering.

Currently, the department offers two programs: a Bachelor of Engineering in Civil Engineering, which spans four years for regular students and three years for lateral entry students, and a Master of Engineering in Structural Engineering, lasting two years. The undergraduate program admits 30 students, split evenly between government and management quotas, requiring applicants to have completed 10+2 in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

The curriculum aims to equip students with skills to thrive as planners, designers, and innovators in technology, while also preparing them for managerial and leadership roles. The vision of the department is to transform students into proficient civil engineers through sustainable practices. This mission includes imparting core knowledge in civil engineering, promoting critical thinking and ethics, and cultivating competencies for career and further education.

The educational objectives for graduates focus on their ability to design sustainable solutions and manage projects effectively. Program outcomes include applying engineering knowledge and problem-solving skills, designing solutions with consideration for health and safety, conducting research-based investigations, and embracing lifelong learning.

Looking ahead, the job outlook for civil engineering in India from 2025 to 2030 appears promising, driven by ongoing government investments in infrastructure projects such as the PM Gati Shakti program and the Smart Cities Mission. As urbanization continues, the need for civil engineers proficient in addressing the challenges associated with population growth and infrastructure demand will remain strong.

At Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering (MSAJCE), students have the opportunity to participate in various clubs and societies, enriching their college experience through diverse activities that foster creativity, teamwork, and personal development.

#### Sports Club
The Sports Club maintains records of the sports events attended by students at various levels, including college, university, regional, state, and national competitions. The club is responsible for submitting an annual report detailing these events and managing budgets. Through organizing various sports activities, the club aims to promote sportsmanship, build friendships, reduce stress, and improve health.

The club's games include outdoor sports like cricket, football, volleyball, basketball, rugby, and badminton, as well as indoor games such as carrom, table tennis, and chess. The motto “Teamwork Makes The Dream Work” encapsulates the club's vision to motivate students to engage in sports at both inter and intra-college levels.

#### Fine Arts Club
The Fine Arts Club at MSAJCE encourages students to explore their artistic talents through various forms of expression, including dance, music, and visual arts. The club aims to provide skill-based training and foster a creative environment where students can confidently share their artistic abilities.

Coordinated by S. Viswajaa, an Assistant Professor in IT, the Fine Arts Club is dedicated to organizing competitions and events that allow students to showcase their talents. Their vision is to inspire students to communicate their thoughts and creativity through visual arts. The club actively participates in external college fests, giving students broader exposure.

#### Science Club
The Science Club seeks to ignite interest in science and technology among students. It comprises students eager to delve into various branches of engineering, mathematics, and science. The club organizes hands-on activities and experiments designed to enhance students' understanding and application of scientific principles in real-life scenarios.

Divided into three sections—Physics, Chemistry, and General—the Science Club aims to promote discussions on recent scientific innovations and host seminars, workshops, exhibitions, and quiz competitions. They also focus on decorating classrooms and laboratories with scientific art created by students themselves.

#### Tamil Mandram
Dedicated to preserving and promoting the Tamil language and culture, the Tamil Mandram focuses on instilling passion and knowledge about Tamil among students. The Mandram aims to enhance students' appreciation for traditional Tamil heritage while integrating modern knowledge.

The club encourages participation in various cultural events to foster engagement with Tamil literature and heritage. Their initiatives include programs like storytelling, debates, and arts that celebrate Tamil culture.

#### Coding Club
The Coding Club emphasizes the importance of programming skills in today’s rapidly evolving landscape. They organize coding contests, workshops on topics such as web development, machine learning, and IoT, and encourage participation in open-source projects.

The club also provides technical assistance to other campus groups and represents the college in intercollegiate competitions. They actively compete in events like ACM-ICPC and Google Code Jam, fostering a robust programming community among students.
Through these clubs and societies, MSAJCE cultivates a vibrant campus life that equips students with skills and experiences essential for their future endeavors.
### Computer Science and Business Systems Program Overview

The B.Tech in Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) is an undergraduate engineering degree that combines core computer science education with business systems and management insights. This modern program, introduced in collaboration with industry leader Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), aims to bridge the gap between traditional computer science education and the evolving requirements of the IT and business sectors, especially relevant in the era of Business 4.0.

The curriculum is tailored to provide students with exposure to emerging topics such as Analytics, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Business Analytics, Financial Management, Economics, and Design Thinking. It also emphasizes the importance of balancing technical skills with an appreciation for the humanities and social sciences. Graduates from this program will be well-prepared for the high-demand job market, possessing both technical expertise and an understanding of business fundamentals.

The primary objective of the CSBS program is to produce graduates who are both technically proficient in computer science and knowledgeable about essential business principles. 
Key features of the program include:

- **Balanced Curriculum**: It incorporates both core computer science subjects, such as Data Structures, Algorithms, and AI/ML, alongside business and management topics like Economics and Financial Management.
- **Industry Collaboration**: The course is designed in partnership with TCS, ensuring relevance to current industry standards and practices.
- **Focus Areas**: Emphasizing problem-solving using computational thinking and decision-making for business analytics, along with soft skills training in communication, teamwork, and entrepreneurial thinking.

Top recruiters for graduates include TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, and various startups in the fintech sector. The program offers several benefits over a traditional Computer Science Engineering (CSE) degree, such as providing interdisciplinary exposure and greater alignment with industry expectations, especially in IT services and consulting roles.

The CSBS program lasts four years for regular entry students and three years for lateral entry students, spread over eight and six semesters, respectively. The total intake is 30 students, divided equally between government and management seats. Students must complete their 10+2 education with a pass in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics to be eligible for admission.

The vision of the CSBS program is to be a center of excellence, transforming students into proficient IT professionals equipped with business skills. Its mission includes the dissemination of core knowledge in Information Science and Business, fostering business acumen and ethical thinking, and developing competencies for career readiness and self-employability.

The program’s educational objectives ensure that graduates are equipped to analyze, design, develop, and test software solutions creatively and sustainably. Graduates are expected to be adept in modern tools and possess managerial skills, ready for leadership roles in diverse sectors.

From 2025 to 2030, the job market for professionals in Computer Science and Business Systems in India is expected to grow significantly. This growth is driven by the country’s increasing digitalization and the rising reliance on AI and business process automation. Key factors contributing to this employment growth include:

- **Digital Transformation of Businesses**: Companies across various sectors are adopting automation and cloud technologies.
- **Growth of AI and Analytics**: Demand for data-driven decision-making continues to rise, necessitating skilled professionals who can integrate and maintain modern technological solutions.
Overall, graduates will find themselves well-positioned in a job market that continues to evolve, demanding a blend of technical skills and business acumen.
### Computer Science and Engineering Department Overview

The Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) program is increasingly popular among engineering students. It emphasizes programming, algorithm analysis, software design and testing, and software engineering. The curriculum integrates both computer hardware and software, fostering a comprehensive understanding of computer systems and their applications in various fields, including education, transportation, healthcare, and more.

CSE is considered interdisciplinary, connecting with areas such as psychology, mathematics, electronics, and bioinformatics, helping students comprehend how computer science intersects with diverse domains.

The Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Computer Science and Engineering typically spans four years for regular students and three years for lateral entry students. The program consists of 8 semesters for full-time students and 6 for lateral entrants. The total intake for the program is 60 seats, divided equally between government and management quotas. Eligibility requires candidates to have completed the 10+2 education system and passed Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics in their qualifying exams.
The Master of Engineering (M.E.) in Computer Science and Engineering is a two-year program consisting of 4 semesters, with an intake of 9 students as per Anna University and AICTE regulations.

The vision of the CSE department is to be a center of excellence that transforms students into proficient engineers through sustainable practices. The mission includes imparting fundamental knowledge and skills via innovative teaching, promoting critical thinking and creativity, and cultivating competencies needed for various career paths.

1. Analyze, design, develop, and test software solutions sustainably.
2. Use modern tools for problem-solving in industrial and societal contexts.
3. Develop managerial and leadership skills for entrepreneurship.

Graduates will also achieve specific program outcomes, such as applying core engineering knowledge to complex problems, designing innovative solutions while considering health and safety, and engaging in life-long learning.

Job opportunities for CSE graduates are abundant, accounting for over 50% of all engineering roles in both public and private sectors. In India, approximately 60% of these jobs are within the IT sector, encompassing various industries such as railways, aviation, and banking, as well as major private companies like TCS, Infosys, and Accenture.

The employment outlook for CSE professionals in India from 2025 to 2030 is optimistic, driven by digital transformation, government initiatives like Digital India, and the rise of smart cities and IoT. The increasing adoption of artificial intelligence and automation across industries underscores the growing demand for skilled computer scientists and engineers.
In conclusion, pursuing a degree in Computer Science and Engineering opens numerous pathways for career advancement and provides a strong foundation in one of today’s most crucial disciplines.

### B.E. in Computer Science and Engineering with Specialization in Cyber Security

The B.E. Computer Science and Engineering program with a specialization in Cyber Security aims to equip students with essential skills to protect computer systems, networks, and data from various cyber threats. This undergraduate program is increasingly relevant due to the growing dependence on digital infrastructure across diverse sectors.

#### Objectives
The primary aim of this program is to create experts in information security, ethical hacking, and digital forensics.

#### Key Features
The curriculum is designed with a focus on security principles, network security, cryptography, ethical hacking, and digital forensics. It includes foundational computer science courses such as data structures, algorithms, and operating systems. The program promotes hands-on learning through labs and simulations for penetration testing, secure coding, and threat analysis. Capstone projects, internships, and real-world problem-solving opportunities further enhance learning. Additionally, legal issues and ethics regarding cyber laws and compliance are embedded in the coursework. Emerging technologies, including security considerations in AI, IoT, cloud computing, and blockchain, are also covered.

#### Career Opportunities
Graduates can expect to find employment in various sectors. Top recruiters include government and defense agencies like DRDO and CERT-IN, IT and cyber security firms such as TCS, Infosys, Accenture, and Palo Alto Networks, as well as banks, healthcare companies, and e-commerce providers.

Opting for a B.E. in Cyber Security provides industry-aligned skills that are highly sought after, ensuring greater job security and compensation opportunities. The degree opens pathways for international careers and contributions to both national and corporate security.

#### Program Structure
The program lasts four years for regular students or three years for lateral entry students, structured into eight and six semesters respectively. A total of 30 seats are available, divided equally between government and management categories. Admission requires candidates to have completed their 10+2 education with passes in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

#### Vision and Mission
The department envisions creating a center of excellence dedicated to transforming students into proficient Cyber Security Specialists through sustainable practices. Its mission includes imparting fundamental knowledge and skills in Cyber Security while fostering critical thinking, ethics, and lifelong learning.

#### Program Educational Objectives (PEOs)
1. Graduates will be prepared to analyze, design, develop, and test software solutions creatively and sustainably.
2. Graduates will be equipped with modern tools for problem-solving and analyzing industrial and societal needs.
3. Graduates will possess managerial and leadership skills to support their careers or start their own businesses.

#### Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs)
- Graduates will develop creative solutions using emerging technologies for real-time industry applications.
- They will apply their knowledge to create innovative software solutions and mobile applications for automation.

#### Program Outcomes (POs)
- Graduates will demonstrate engineering knowledge and apply foundational sciences to real-world engineering problems.
- They will be skilled in identifying and analyzing complex engineering issues using systematic methodologies.
- Graduates will design solutions considering public health, safety, and ethical responsibilities.
- They will apply research methods to investigate complex problems and deliver valid conclusions.
- Graduates will be proficient in using modern engineering tools and understand their limitations.
- They will recognize and address societal, health, legal, and cultural issues related to professional engineering.
- The curriculum emphasizes sustainability and the environmental impact of engineering solutions.
- Ethical considerations and professional responsibilities will be a focal point of their education.
- They will be trained to work effectively both individually and in diverse teams.
- Communication skills will be honed for effective interaction with both engineering peers and the broader community.
- Graduates will understand project management principles to lead and manage projects effectively.
- The program encourages lifelong learning and adaptation in response to technological advancements.

#### Future Job Market Trends
The Cyber Security sector in India is projected to undergo significant growth by 2025-2030, driven by the rapid digitization of businesses and the increasing complexity of cyber threats. Key factors contributing to employment growth include escalating cyber threats, government initiatives like the Personal Data Protection Bill, and an ongoing shift toward cloud computing and IoT. All of these trends underscore the need for skilled cyber security professionals to develop secure systems and protect data from breaches.

The university offers a variety of programs across different academic departments, ensuring students have access to diverse fields of study. Each department plays a crucial role in supporting the educational objectives of the institution, providing resources and guidance to enhance the learning experience.
### Overview of the B.Tech Program in ECE with Specialization in Advanced Communication Technology

The B.Tech program in Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) with a specialization in Advanced Communication Technology is designed under the guidelines of the National Education Policy (NEP). Its primary aim is to develop industry-ready professionals capable of tackling global challenges in the rapidly evolving field of communication, especially with the advent of 5G and 6G technologies.

Students in this program will explore a variety of modern communication systems, including Wireless and Optical Communication, RF and Microwave Communication, and advanced technologies such as Massive MIMO, Beamforming, Device-to-Device communication, and Millimeter Wave technologies. The curriculum also covers essential topics like IoT, Cybersecurity, and Information Theory, equipping graduates with practical skills that are in high demand across various industries.

The B.Tech program lasts four years for regular entry and three years for lateral entry, with eight semesters for regular students and six semesters for lateral entrants. The program has a total intake of 30 seats, divided evenly between government and management quotas.
Prospective students must have completed their 10+2 education, securing a passing grade in subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

The department aims to be a centre of excellence, transforming students into proficient communication engineers through sustainable practices. Its educational objectives include imparting fundamental knowledge, fostering critical thinking, and nurturing creativity and ethical values essential for professional success.

Graduates of this program will be prepared to design, develop, and analyze software solutions creatively. They will gain skills in modern tools for problem-solving and gain managerial competencies, enabling them to launch their own ventures.

Key Program Outcomes include the ability to apply engineering knowledge to solve complex problems, design solutions considering societal and environmental impacts, and engage in lifelong learning to keep pace with technological changes.

Graduates can pursue various job roles such as Design Engineer, ASIC Engineer, Embedded Engineer, Network Support Engineer, Software Engineer, and Quality Analyst. Each role requires a unique skill set, ranging from designing new products to ensuring the smooth operation of communication systems and developing innovative software applications.

In conclusion, the B.Tech ECE program with a specialization in Advanced Communication Technology not only equips students with vital technical skills but also empowers them with the competencies necessary for diverse career paths in an increasingly digitized world.
### Electronics Engineering with Specialization in VLSI Design & Technology

The Department of Electronics Engineering offers a Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) program with a specialization in VLSI Design & Technology. This program responds to the increasing demand for integrated circuits and sensors in the rapidly evolving digital economy. The global semiconductor market, having reached $590 billion in 2021, is projected to grow to $1 trillion by 2030. This surge is driven by advancements in technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Electric Vehicles, 5G Networks, and the Internet of Things. As India currently imports integrated chips worth billions of dollars, there is a critical need to bridge the gap through indigenous manufacturing.

The Government of India has recognized the importance of establishing a robust semiconductor ecosystem, paving the way for various initiatives. In December 2021, the Indian government announced a Semiconductor scheme with an investment of ₹76,000 crore to support the "Aatma Nirbhar Bharat" initiative. A significant focus is on training the required manpower, as an estimated workforce of 85,000 professionals in fields related to VLSI design, semiconductor manufacturing, and various engineering streams will be needed in the next five to seven years.

The B.Tech program in Electronics Engineering with a specialization in VLSI Design & Technology is structured to equip students with industry-ready skills. The curriculum addresses core subjects such as:

- Semiconductor device fundamentals
- Analog and Digital circuits
- VLSI circuits and design
- IC design technology
- Testing and packaging of semiconductor devices
- Usage of Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools
This comprehensive approach prepares graduates for diverse roles within the semiconductor industry.
Graduates of the program can pursue various roles in the semiconductor sector, including:

- Design Engineer
- Verification Engineer
- CAD Engineer
- Application Engineer
- Analog Circuit Designer

With companies like Intel, IBM, and AMD actively seeking VLSI talent, prospects are promising. Starting salaries range from ₹3 lakhs to ₹20 lakhs per annum, with average earnings around ₹14 lakhs. In core multinational firms, initial salaries can reach between ₹30 lakhs and ₹80 lakhs.

- **Degree**: B.Tech in Electronics Engineering with Specialization in VLSI Design & Technology.
- **Duration**: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry).
- **Number of Semesters**: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry).
- **Intake**: Total of 30 seats (15 through Government quota and 15 through Management quota).
- **Eligibility**: Candidates must have completed the 10+2 education system with a pass in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.
This program is dedicated to fostering human resources essential for the semiconductor industry, aligning with national growth objectives in electronics manufacturing and design.

The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering focuses on utilizing electronic concepts to transfer information effectively. This field integrates scientific and mathematical principles, fostering innovation to solve practical problems. The rapid advancement of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) has opened up new domains such as healthcare, automation, remote sensing, and advanced communication.

The Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electronics and Communication Engineering lasts four years for regular students and three years for lateral entry students. The program comprises eight semesters for regular applicants and six for lateral entrants, with a total intake of 60 students—30 from government seats and 30 from management seats. Eligibility requires completion of the 10+2 education system with a pass in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

Graduates from this program are well-qualified for careers in various sectors, including positions as systems engineers, research and development professionals, software engineers, and maintenance engineers. They can find job opportunities in companies such as Aagree Equipments Pvt. Ltd., Apps Daily Solution, and O M Innovation Services.
For further studies, graduates can pursue a Master of Engineering (M.E.), Master of Technology (M.Tech), or Master of Business Administration (MBA).

The department aims to be a center of excellence in training proficient Electronics and Communication Engineers through sustainable practices. Its mission includes imparting core knowledge, fostering critical thinking and creativity, and equipping students with the competencies necessary for career development and self-employment.

Graduates will be prepared to:
1. Design components and systems with creativity and sustainability in mind.
2. Employ modern tools for problem-solving in industrial and societal contexts.
3. Develop managerial and leadership skills for professional success, including entrepreneurship.

Students will acquire several outcomes, including:
- Mastery in engineering knowledge and problem analysis.
- Capability to design effective solutions while considering societal and environmental impacts.
- Proficiency in modern tool usage and effective communication within diverse teams.
- Understanding of professional ethics and the importance of lifelong learning.

The job market for Electronics and Communication Engineers shows promising trends for 2025-2030. There is a significant demand driven by the rollout of 5G and 6G technologies, as well as advancements in signal processing, antenna design, and semiconductor industries.

Key sectors projected to grow include:
- **Telecommunications**: Anticipated growth of 10–12% annually, focusing on RF design and protocol testing.
- **Semiconductors/VLSI**: Expected growth rate of 12–15%, particularly in RTL and physical design roles.
- **Embedded Systems and IoT**: Projected growth of 15–18%, emphasizing job opportunities in smart technologies and automation.
Graduates will require core skills in microcontrollers, programming, and various engineering tools to meet the evolving demands of the industry.
### Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Overview

The Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering focuses on designing, installing, and operating a range of equipment and machines, from small motors to large generators and turbines. Electrical engineering jobs make up more than 25% of all available engineering roles, with about 30% of government jobs in India being in this sector. Graduates can enter diverse fields such as Computer Science and Engineering (CSE), Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE), Information and Communication Engineering (ICE), Robotics, Mechanical Engineering, and Healthcare. However, professionals from other engineering branches cannot transition into electrical engineering roles.

With the imminent boom in the electric vehicle sector, the demand for electrical engineers is expected to rise significantly, contributing greatly to India's development and economy. The Indian government is promoting renewable energy sources such as solar and wind to reduce carbon footprints and enhance economic growth.

The Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical and Electronics Engineering is structured over four years, comprising eight semesters for regular students and six semesters for those entering laterally. The program has a total intake of 30 students, divided equally between government and management seats. To be eligible, students must have completed their 10+2 education, passing exams in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

Graduates can pursue various career paths, including roles such as circuit designers, energy efficiency engineers, power system engineers, research engineers, electrical safety engineers, and power plant engineers. Those interested in further studies can opt for M.E., M.Tech, or M.B.A programs.

The department aims to be a center of excellence, transforming students into proficient electrical and electronics engineers using sustainable practices. The mission includes imparting fundamental knowledge and skills through innovative teaching methodologies, fostering critical thinking and creativity, and preparing students for careers, higher education, and self-employability.

1. Graduates will design electrical and electronics components and systems with creativity and sustainability.
2. Graduates will utilize modern tools for problem-solving, addressing industrial and societal needs.
3. Graduates will develop managerial and leadership skills for careers and entrepreneurship.

The program equips students with the following abilities:
- **Engineering Knowledge**: Application of engineering fundamentals to solve complex problems.
- **Problem Analysis**: Identifying and analyzing engineering problems effectively.
- **Design/Development**: Creating solutions that consider public health, safety, and environmental impacts.
- **Investigations**: Conducting research and utilizing methods to draw valid conclusions.
- **Modern Tool Usage**: Selecting and applying modern engineering tools and techniques.
- **Societal Awareness**: Assessing societal and ethical issues related to engineering practice.
- **Sustainability**: Understanding the environmental impacts of engineering solutions.
- **Ethics**: Commitment to ethical principles in engineering.
- **Teamwork**: Functioning effectively in diverse teams.
- **Communication**: Clearly communicating complex engineering activities.
- **Project Management**: Applying management principles to engineering projects.
- **Life-long Learning**: Engaging in continuous learning to adapt to technological changes.

Electrical and electronics engineering graduates are poised for job opportunities across both public and private sectors, with positions available in manufacturing, consultancy, electrical components, power generation, and green energy companies in India.

**Future Job Growth (2025–2030)**: 
- Key growth drivers include energy transition and renewable energy, with a target of 500 GW from solar and wind sources.
- The electric vehicle market is expected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 40%, increasing demand in power electronics and battery management systems.
- The deployment of smart grids and IoT technologies is creating a need for engineers specialized in data analytics and AI.
- Government initiatives for semiconductor and electronics manufacturing will boost job demand in areas like PCB design and VLSI.

This anticipated growth illustrates the increasing relevance and opportunities within the field of electrical and electronics engineering, aligning with national economic goals and technological advancements.

At MSAJCE, both boys and girls have access to separate hostels, which provide a comfortable "home away from home." The boys' hostel accommodates 480 students, while the girls' hostel can house 210. Each room is well-furnished, featuring a cot, mattress, pillows, bedspreads, individual cupboards, and a study area with a chair and lamp. Boys' rooms typically accommodate two students, while girls' rooms can hold three.

The boys' hostel includes a hygienic canteen for dining, an entertainment hall with an LCD TV, and facilities for indoor games. Students also have access to a reading room stocked with newspapers and popular magazines. Additionally, the hostel offers Wi-Fi and landline telephone services, and the main library and computer center remain open until 7:00 PM to support students' studies.

The girls' hostel, located five kilometers from the main campus in Sholinganallur, boasts similar amenities but with dedicated bath and toilet facilities in each room. The Information Technology Department plays a crucial role in today's world, substantially affecting various aspects of human life. The current job landscape in India offers numerous opportunities in the IT sector, both domestically and internationally. Additionally, the program outcomes encompass a comprehensive understanding of engineering principles, critical problem analysis, effective communication, project management, and lifelong learning.
The faculty within the department includes 16 members, with a strong emphasis on qualifications and regular association. Notable faculty include:

- Dr. Ammu Qudsiya A., Associate Professor, who joined on December 22, 2022, and holds a Ph.D.
- Mrs. Senthil Lakshmi, Assistant Professor, who has been with the department since June 24, 2019, and holds an M.Phil.
- Dr. Kavitha J., Assistant Professor, who joined on July 24, 2023, with a Ph.D.
- Dr. Balakrishnan A., an Associate Professor since July 15, 2013, also holds a Ph.D.
This diverse and qualified faculty team is dedicated to fostering an enriching educational atmosphere to prepare students for challenges in their future careers.
### Social Services at Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering (MSAJCE)

**National Service Scheme (NSS)**  
Established in 2001, the NSS at MSAJCE aims to promote selfless service and the spirit of social service among students. With over 100 active members from the first and second years, the NSS unit engages in various community betterment efforts in and around Chennai. Activities include blood donation camps, health and hygiene awareness programs, consumer rights initiatives, environmental protection campaigns, and AIDS awareness programs.

The primary objectives of NSS include working closely with the community, enhancing their understanding of communal issues, applying academic knowledge to real-world challenges, developing democratic leadership skills, and bridging the educational gap between various societal strata. NSS volunteers are expected to foster community relationships, identify local needs, plan and execute programs, and maintain systematic records of their activities. Each volunteer is required to wear the NSS badge during service.

NSS organizes significant events throughout the year, commemorating important national days like Youth Day, Independence Day, and Republic Day, along with various health programs, awareness camps, and motivational initiatives.

**Youth Red Cross (YRC) Club**  
The YRC at MSAJCE, formed in 2014, is a branch of the Indian Red Cross. It aims to inculcate a sense of social welfare in students while preparing them to contribute meaningfully to society. The club currently comprises around 100 active members, who are trained to manage the group and elect their own leaders.

YRC's objectives include conducting health and social awareness programs, promoting humanitarian service, providing first aid training, and fostering a sense of duty among the youth. The club encourages a friendly, non-discriminatory atmosphere among its members.

**Unnat Bharat Abhiyan (UBA)**  
Inspired by Gandhi’s vision for rural development, the UBA aims to create self-sufficient village republics using local resources and eco-friendly technologies. The initiative focuses on connecting educational institutions with rural communities to address development challenges through participatory processes.

**NSS Activities**  
In 2023, the NSS organized a series of awareness programs and health camps. Key events included a Tobacco Awareness Rally, Eye Screening Camp, and International Yoga Day celebration, targeted at fostering health consciousness among the community.
Past activities included various health camps, motivational speeches, awareness rallies on cancer, blood donation drives, and educational programs addressing pressing issues such as drug awareness.

**YRC and UBA Initiatives**  
The YRC and UBA continue to undertake projects that support community needs. Noteworthy activities include medical camps for local residents, awareness programs on environmental concerns, and various cleaning initiatives in local schools.

In 2022, significant events took place, such as blood donation camps and awareness programs addressing social issues like drug abuse and sexual harassment. Other initiatives involved clean-up drives and programs focusing on health and safety.
These programs demonstrate the commitment of MSAJCE students to community service and development, reflecting the college's ethos of nurturing socially responsible citizens.

The Department of Physical Education at MSAJCE emphasizes the importance of sports activities for students, recognizing their role in enhancing physical strength, stamina, and overall well-being. The college has established comprehensive facilities to promote physical education, as engaging in sports contributes to character development and academic performance. Research shows that regular exercise enhances blood flow to the brain, improving concentration, creativity, problem-solving abilities, and learning speed.

Dr. K. P. Santhosh Nathan serves as the Director of Physical Education, holding multiple qualifications including a Ph.D. and various degrees in Physical Education. He can be reached via email at ped.santhosh@msajce-edu.in. Supporting him is Mr. M. Janakiraman, the Assistant Director of Physical Education, who possesses a Bachelor's and a Master's degree in Physical Education, as well as specialized training in cricket.

The department aims to provide opportunities for talent identification and development, encouraging students to excel in inter-collegiate and university competitions. It fosters a unique culture of physical fitness, competitiveness, and camaraderie within MSAJCE.

To further support sports enthusiasts, the college offers admission through a sports quota and scholarships for participants at the district, state, and national levels. MSAJCE boasts excellent infrastructure for various sports, including basketball, football, cricket (including nets), yoga, kabaddi, volleyball, table tennis, carrom, chess, track and field, and kho kho.

Additionally, the college has a well-equipped gymnasium available for both students and faculty. This fitness center features modern equipment such as a multi-gym, leg extension and preacher curl benches, a cable crossover machine, various types of weights, and spin bikes, catering to fitness enthusiasts and athletes alike.

The department actively participates in annual sports activities, including competitions hosted by Anna University, zonal tournaments, and invites to other sports events. Key events organized by MSAJCE include the Annual Sports Day, Mohamed Sathak Trophy for football, BSM Trophy for cricket, and the Fit India Cyclothon, which encourage widespread participation and physical fitness among students.

The Technology Centre was established in the academic year 2019-2020 to align with the vision and mission of MSAJCE, aiming to make students deployable and equip them with skills relevant to emerging industrial demands. The courses and training offered are developed following the guidelines provided by AICTE and the India Skill Report 2020.

The Technology Centres are equipped with state-of-the-art facilities that foster an ambient environment conducive to research and innovation. Each centre collaborates with various industry and government organizations, providing students with opportunities for certifications, onsite and offsite projects, as well as internships. Partnering industries include notable names like Automation Anywhere, Godrej Inc, Ford, Palo Alto Systems, Amazon Web Services, National Instruments, CISCO Academy, Altair, OpenWave Computing, Levergent Technologies, and Propeller Technologies.
There are several specialized Technology and Skill Development Centres across different domains.

In the fields of Mechanical and Civil Engineering, the centres include:
- The Centre for 3D Printing and Additive Manufacturing
- The Centre for Computer-Aided Engineering (CAE)
- The Centre for Industrial Robotics
- The Centre for Non-Destructive Testing (NDT)
- The Centre for Building Information Modelling.

In Electrical and Electronics Engineering, the following centres are available:
- The Centre for Embedded & Internet of Things (IoT)
- The Centre for UAV and Drone Technology
- The Centre for Godrej Disha Skill Development
- The Centre for Bosch Industry Institute Collaboration
- The Centre for E-Mobility
- The Centre for Renewable Energy
- The Centre for APJ Abdul Kalam Innovation.

Finally, in the fields of Computer Science and Information Technology, the centres comprise:
- The Centre for Bot Lab and Robotics Process Automation
- The Centre for Cisco Networking Academy
- The Centre for Code Tantra, focusing on coding learning
- The Centre for Gaming and Augmented/Virtual Reality
- The Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
- The Centre for Blockchain Technology
- The Centre for Mobile and Web Application Development.
These centres collectively aim to provide students with relevant skills to meet the demands of the modern industrial landscape.

The vision of Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering (MSAJCE) is to become a leading institution for higher education and research. This goal is driven by innovative teaching and sustainable practices aimed at fulfilling both industrial and societal needs.

The mission of MSAJCE is threefold: first, it focuses on providing quality technical education that transforms students into professionally competent technocrats. Second, the college emphasizes the all-round sustainable development of students to prepare them for successful careers and self-employment. Lastly, it aims to instill critical thinking, professional ethics, and civic responsibilities by fostering core values among its students.

In support of these ideals, MSAJCE is committed to creating quality professionals through innovative teaching and research, maintaining strong industrial relations, and nurturing human values and ethics. This commitment is structured on a basis of continual improvement to meet the evolving needs of industry and society.
